output: Implemented the marker-consume path in `src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py` and the curator marker registrations in `src/features/curating/curator.py`. `on_raw_reaction_remove` now pops and consumes bot-initiated markers before any self-removal write, records `user_self_removal` only when no marker exists, marks suspicion only for `🤮`/`👎`/`😭`, and sends the admin DM in a fire-and-forget task with its own try/except. The four curator `❌` reaction-removal sites now register `bot_curator_reject` markers immediately before removal, with no direct moderation-decision write added to curator. Verified both modules with `python -m py_compile` and wrote the batch checkpoint to `.megaplan/plans/design-and-implement-a-20260419-1105/execution_batch_4.json`.
deviations: `src/features/curating/curator.py` does not have a `self.bot` attribute; `ArtCurator` is itself a bot subclass, so the four marker insertions use `self.get_cog('ReactorCog')` instead of `self.bot.get_cog('ReactorCog')`. No broader refactor was introduced.

task_updates.executor_notes: Updated `on_raw_reaction_remove` in `src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py` to pop and consume `_bot_initiated_removals[(message.id, user.id, emoji_str)]` before any self-removal write, emitting exactly one moderation-decision row from the marker path and returning immediately so no duplicate `user_self_removal` row is written. Verified the no-marker path writes `classification='user_self_removal'`, sets `is_suspicious` only for `🤮`/`👎`/`😭`, and triggers an admin DM via a fire-and-forget task whose try/except is separate from the DB write path; the DM body includes the reactor mention, emoji, `message.jump_url`, and the 200-character snippet.

Inserted the curator marker-registration block immediately before all four `await message.remove_reaction('❌', user)` sites in `src/features/curating/curator.py` and verified there are exactly four such registrations. No direct `record_moderation_decision` call was added to curator; the remove handler in `ReactorCog` remains the sole writer for the curator path. Minor implementation deviation: `ArtCurator` is itself a bot subclass and has no `self.bot`, so the insertion uses `self.get_cog('ReactorCog')` rather than `self.bot.get_cog(...)`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed the marker-present branch in `on_raw_reaction_remove` pops the marker, writes exactly one moderation-decision row with the marker classification/reason, and returns before any `user_self_removal` write. The no-marker branch writes `user_self_removal`, sets `is_suspicious` only for `🤮`/`👎`/`😭`, and sends the admin DM in a separate fire-and-forget task wrapped in its own try/except with reactor mention, emoji, jump URL, and the 200-char snippet.

Verified all four curator `await message.remove_reaction('❌', user)` sites are immediately preceded by `register_bot_removal(..., 'bot_curator_reject', reason='curator_reject')`, and no direct `record_moderation_decision` call was added in `curator.py`. Because `ArtCurator` has no `self.bot` attribute, the safe local equivalent is `self.get_cog('ReactorCog')`.
